evals.name: fresh-idea-intake
evals.prompt: I'm thinking about building an app that helps people find study buddies at their university. Like Tinder but for studying.
evals.expected_output: Phase 0 repo scan launched in background, 1-2 intake questions, hard STOP

assertions.name: one_or_two_questions
assertions.description: Asks 1-2 questions max, not a wall of questions

assertions.name: no_research_sprint_turn1
assertions.description: Does NOT dispatch research agents on the very first turn — intake only

assertions.name: hard_stop
assertions.description: Ends with a question and waits — does NOT proceed to synthesize or generate suggestions

assertions.name: repo_scan_launched
assertions.description: Mentions or silently launches a repo/codebase scan agent in background

assertions.name: follows_energy
assertions.description: Asks about the most interesting/emphasized part of the idea, not a generic checklist question

assertions.name: no_skill_questions
assertions.description: Does NOT ask about the user's technical experience or skill level

evals.name: fresh-idea-turn2-research
evals.prompt: It's for college students who want to find people in the same classes to study with. Matching based on courses, study style, and schedule availability.
evals.expected_output: First research sprint dispatched (7 agents + synthesizer), synthesized suggestions with citations, challenge, 1-2 questions, STOP
evals.multi_turn_context: Turn 2. Turn 1: user said 'building study buddy app like Tinder for studying', skill asked intake questions, user now responds with details.

assertions.name: seven_agents_dispatched
assertions.description: Dispatches 7 parallel research agents (web, github, competitive, stack, architecture, pitfall, UX) plus synthesizer — not fewer in standard mode

assertions.name: real_citations
assertions.description: At least 3 unique URLs to real repos/articles/products in the synthesis

assertions.name: suggestions_with_evidence
assertions.description: Presents numbered suggestions (3-5) with real-world evidence backing each one

assertions.name: recommendation_labeled
assertions.description: Suggestion #1 is labeled RECOMMENDED with rationale

assertions.name: challenge_present
assertions.description: Includes at least one challenge/pushback after suggestions — as a statement, NOT a question

assertions.name: ends_with_question
assertions.description: Ends with 1-2 targeted questions via AskUserQuestion to drive next research sprint

assertions.name: hard_stop
assertions.description: Does NOT continue past the questions — waits for user response

assertions.name: depth_is_broad
assertions.description: Research queries are landscape-level (major approaches, who's done this) not implementation-specific

assertions.name: synthesizer_output
assertions.description: Shows consensus/contested/unique findings structure from synthesizer

evals.name: turn3-deeper-research
evals.prompt: I like option 2 — the React Native approach with Firebase. But I'm worried about the matching algorithm complexity. Also we need to handle the cold-start problem when a new university joins.
evals.expected_output: Second research sprint (deeper, focused on RN+Firebase+matching), new suggestions building on prior, new challenges, new questions
evals.multi_turn_context: Turn 3. Prior turns: user described study buddy app, first research sprint found 5 approaches, user now picks one and raises two specific concerns.

assertions.name: seven_agents_dispatched
assertions.description: Dispatches 7 parallel research agents again — every turn gets a research sprint

assertions.name: research_is_deeper
assertions.description: Search queries target matching algorithms and cold-start specifically, NOT broad 'study buddy app' landscape again

assertions.name: builds_on_prior
assertions.description: References prior turn's findings — does not re-present the same 5 approaches

assertions.name: new_citations
assertions.description: At least 2 URLs not seen in prior turns — fresh research, not recycled

assertions.name: addresses_user_concerns
assertions.description: Suggestions specifically address matching algorithm complexity AND cold-start problem

assertions.name: challenge_present
assertions.description: Challenges the user on at least one assumption or pushes back on scope

assertions.name: ends_with_question
assertions.description: Ends with 1-2 questions that unlock the NEXT research vector

assertions.name: hard_stop
assertions.description: Does NOT continue past the questions

assertions.name: prior_decision_logged
assertions.description: Records 'React Native + Firebase chosen' in prior decisions log — does NOT re-ask about tech stack

evals.name: brain-dump-intake
evals.prompt: help me build all the suggestions in this chat: [brain dump about eng-buddy]
evals.expected_output: Path B structured extraction with repo context, confirmation gate, no research yet

assertions.name: decided_section
assertions.description: Contains a 'Decided' or 'Decisions already made' section

assertions.name: open_questions_section
assertions.description: Contains an 'Open questions' or 'Gaps' section

assertions.name: no_basic_questions
assertions.description: Does NOT ask basic 'what are you building?' questions already answered by the paste

assertions.name: confirmation_gate
assertions.description: Ends with a confirmation question before proceeding to research

assertions.name: no_research_sprint
assertions.description: Does NOT dispatch research agents or present suggestions on this turn

assertions.name: hard_stop
assertions.description: Stops after asking for confirmation — does not proceed

evals.name: brain-dump-turn2-research
evals.prompt: Yeah that looks right, go ahead and research it
evals.expected_output: First research sprint in brain dump mode: novelty map, suggestions with citations, challenges
evals.multi_turn_context: Turn 2 of brain dump. Turn 1: user pasted eng-buddy brain dump, skill extracted structured summary, user now confirms.

assertions.name: seven_agents_dispatched
assertions.description: Dispatches 7 parallel research agents searching for specific brain dump claims

assertions.name: novelty_map_present
assertions.description: Contains a Novelty Map table with solved/partially solved/novel verdicts

assertions.name: real_citations
assertions.description: At least 5 unique URLs to real repos/articles/products

assertions.name: brain_dump_claims_researched
assertions.description: Agents searched for specific architectural claims from the dump, not just broad topic searches

assertions.name: challenge_present
assertions.description: At least one challenge/pushback raised as a statement

assertions.name: ends_with_question
assertions.description: Ends with 1-2 targeted questions via AskUserQuestion

assertions.name: hard_stop
assertions.description: Does NOT proceed past questions

evals.name: phase3-gate
evals.prompt: Ok I think I know what I want. Let's turn this into a plan.
evals.expected_output: Spec self-review + pre-mortem runs, vision summary with risk mitigations for approval, NOT the full plan yet
evals.multi_turn_context: Turn 6+ of brainstorming. Previous turns explored study-buddy app, settled on React Native + Firebase, matching algorithm, cold-start solution. User now wants to move to planning.

assertions.name: spec_self_review
assertions.description: Runs spec self-review checking for placeholders, contradictions, scope gaps, ambiguity

assertions.name: pre_mortem_runs
assertions.description: Runs pre-mortem stress test generating failure scenarios with mitigations

assertions.name: vision_summary
assertions.description: Presents a brief 'here's what we've landed on' summary including key decisions AND top risks

assertions.name: canonical_references
assertions.description: Includes canonical references section with links to key sources from research

assertions.name: approval_gate
assertions.description: Asks for explicit confirmation before proceeding to full plan generation

assertions.name: does_not_dump_full_plan
assertions.description: Does NOT generate the entire task breakdown, agent assignments, and wave structure in this message

assertions.name: references_plan_template
assertions.description: Reads or references references/plan-template.md for plan generation

evals.name: assumption-audit-activation
evals.prompt: I want to build a real-time collaborative whiteboard where multiple users can draw simultaneously. It needs to sync instantly across devices.
evals.expected_output: Intake question, and on turn 2-3 the assumption audit module should activate to challenge 'instantly' and 'real-time' assumptions
evals.multi_turn_context: Turn 2-3. User has described a collaborative whiteboard with real-time sync. The assumption audit should catch assumptions about 'instantly' (network latency exists), 'real-time' (what tolerance?), and synchronization approach.

assertions.name: assumption_audit_activates
assertions.description: Runs the First Principles Assumption Audit module

assertions.name: crackable_assumptions_found
assertions.description: Identifies at least one crackable assumption (e.g., 'instantly' is a design choice, not a requirement)

assertions.name: five_levels_analyzed
assertions.description: Examines assumptions at surface, process, structural, cultural, and fundamental levels

assertions.name: user_can_respond
assertions.description: Presents assumptions as keep/challenge/ignore choices via AskUserQuestion

evals.name: discuss-mode-activation
evals.prompt: I know exactly what I want. Here's my spec: Build a REST API endpoint POST /api/v1/recordings that accepts multipart form data with an audio file (max 500MB), stores it in S3 with a UUID filename, creates a database record with metadata (duration, format, size, created_at), and returns the record ID with a signed URL for playback. Use Express.js with multer for uploads, pg for Postgres, and aws-sdk for S3.
evals.expected_output: Discuss mode activates: parses spec, identifies gray areas using SEE/CALL/RUN/READ/ORGANIZE heuristics, asks targeted questions

assertions.name: discuss_mode_detected
assertions.description: Recognizes this is a detailed spec and activates Discuss Mode instead of standard brainstorm

assertions.name: spec_parsed
assertions.description: Extracts key components: endpoint, storage, database, response format

assertions.name: gray_areas_found
assertions.description: Identifies gray areas: auth, rate limiting, error handling for S3 failures, max concurrent uploads, file validation

assertions.name: uses_heuristics
assertions.description: Applies SEE/CALL/RUN/READ/ORGANIZE categorization to find unknowns

assertions.name: targeted_questions
assertions.description: Asks 3-5 specific questions about gray areas, NOT basic 'what are you building' questions

assertions.name: no_broad_research
assertions.description: Does NOT launch a broad 7-agent landscape research sprint — goes straight to targeted analysis

evals.name: scope-guardrail
evals.prompt: Oh actually, while we're at it, we should also add a social feed where users can post their study notes and other students can comment on them.
evals.expected_output: Acknowledges the idea, adds to Deferred Ideas, redirects to current scope
evals.multi_turn_context: Turn 4 of study buddy app brainstorm. Core scope is matching + scheduling. User now wants to add a social feed — this is scope creep.

assertions.name: acknowledges_idea
assertions.description: Does NOT dismiss the idea — acknowledges it's worth building

assertions.name: defers_to_future
assertions.description: Adds the social feed to a Deferred Ideas list

assertions.name: redirects_to_scope
assertions.description: Steers back to the current core scope (matching + scheduling)

assertions.name: does_not_incorporate
assertions.description: Does NOT start researching social feeds or adding it to the current plan

evals.name: anti-rationalization-check
evals.prompt: This is pretty straightforward, just generate the plan already. We don't need more research.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT skip to plan generation. Checks anti-rationalization table, explains why process matters, continues the brainstorm loop.
evals.multi_turn_context: Turn 3 of a complex brainstorm. Only 2 research sprints completed. User is impatient but the skill has a hard gate requiring explicit readiness AND research saturation.

assertions.name: does_not_skip
assertions.description: Does NOT immediately generate a plan — honors the hard gate

assertions.name: explains_value
assertions.description: Briefly explains what remaining research could surface, without being preachy

assertions.name: offers_quick_mode
assertions.description: Offers Quick Mode as a compromise if the user truly wants speed

assertions.name: continues_loop
assertions.description: Runs at least one more research sprint to check for saturation

evals.name: plan-quality-verification
evals.expected_output: Runs plan checker agent before saving. Fixes any issues found. Then saves.
evals.multi_turn_context: Phase 3, final section approved. Plan has been presented incrementally and user approved all 3 sections. Now saving.

assertions.name: plan_checker_runs
assertions.description: Dispatches a plan checker agent to validate the plan before saving

assertions.name: nyquist_validated
assertions.description: Every task in the plan has an automated verify command

assertions.name: discovery_levels_tagged
assertions.description: Every task has a discovery level (L0-L3)

assertions.name: canonical_references_present
assertions.description: Plan includes a canonical references section

assertions.name: deferred_ideas_present
assertions.description: Plan includes deferred ideas from scope guardrail

assertions.name: handoff_prompt
assertions.description: Provides /ftm-executor handoff command after saving
